title: teutliopsis rosea
intro: Teutliopsis rosea, also known as Mexican Prickly Poppy, is a flowering plant species native to Mexico and parts of Central America. It belongs to the genus Teutliopsis and is characterized by its bright yellow or orange flowers and spiny stems. Traditionally, Teutliopsis rosea has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been used to treat various health issues such as wounds, fever, and digestive problems. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, which are often used to alleviate symptoms of colds, sore throats, and respiratory issues, as well as to promote overall well-being.
